task_completion.description: The agent adjusts the axial slice to display the orbits.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The axial (top-down) viewport shows a cross-section of the skull with the two circular cavities of the eye sockets (orbits) clearly visible at the front.

task_completion.description: The agent exports the axial view to /home/ga/Documents/axial_orbits.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or export window is visible with the filename 'axial_orbits.png' and the destination folder set to Documents.

task_completion.description: The agent adjusts the sagittal slice to the midline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The sagittal (side) viewport shows a midline profile of the skull, with the nasal cavity and the brainstem/spinal cord canal clearly visible.

task_completion.description: The agent exports the sagittal view to /home/ga/Documents/sagittal_midline.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or export window is visible with the filename 'sagittal_midline.png' and the destination folder set to Documents.

task_completion.description: The agent adjusts the coronal slice to display the petrous temporal bones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The coronal (front-to-back) viewport shows a cross-section of the skull where the dense, thick petrous parts of the temporal bones (near the base of the skull/ear region) are visible.

task_completion.description: The agent exports the coronal view to /home/ga/Documents/coronal_petrous.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or export window is visible with the filename 'coronal_petrous.png' and the destination folder set to Documents.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the medical imaging software GUI to scroll through slices and locate the correct anatomical views.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking or scrolling within the 2D slice viewports of the medical imaging software (e.g., InVesalius) to change the visible anatomy.

integrity.description: The agent used the software's built-in export functionality to generate the PNG files.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks on an 'Export', 'Save Picture', or similar button within the software GUI, opening a native file save dialog.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or text editors to create dummy files.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'touch', 'cp', or 'echo' being used to create the required .png files in the Documents folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires exporting three specific anatomical views from the CT Cranium DICOM dataset. The expected files are: 1) /home/ga/Documents/axial_orbits.png (Axial view showing orbits), 2) /home/ga/Documents/sagittal_midline.png (Sagittal midline view showing nasal cavity and brainstem), and 3) /home/ga/Documents/coronal_petrous.png (Coronal view of petrous bones). Each exported image must be at least 30 KB in size.
